actions-list.header: On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Roll Call 209
actions-list.actionItem: 2/3 Yea and Nay vote pending.

Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 8428 — "To amend title 5, United States Code, to establish a mandatory antifraud and improper payment training program for Federal program administrators, to provide for the availability of such training to State and local entities administering Federally funded programs, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by the Yeas and Nays (2/3 required): (Roll no. 208).
Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 8466 — "To require certain agencies to develop plans for internal control in the event of an emergency or crisis, and for other purposes."
The Chair announced the unfinished business was the question on motions to suspend the rules which had been debated earlier and on which further proceedings had been postponed.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of June 8.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for approximately 6:30 P.M. today.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 5248.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 5248 — "To ensure the alignment of economic and foreign policies, to position the Department of State to reflect that economic security is national security, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6338.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 6338 — "To require the imposition of sanctions with respect to foreign persons and foreign vessels that engage in illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 7668.
Considered under suspension of the rules.
Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 7668 — "To require a report on Russian and Chinese intelligence assets in Georgia, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 8665.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 8665 — "To require the implementation of a strategy to encourage foreign partners to participate in the foreign military sales and direct commercial sales processes on a multinational basis, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 7037.
Considered under suspension of the rules.
Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 7037 — "To promote United States and allied energy and mineral security, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6230.
Considered under suspension of the rules.
Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 6230 — "To designate certain individuals as specially designated global terrorists."
The title of the measure was amended. Agreed to without objection.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6297.
Considered under suspension of the rules.
Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 6297 — "To require the Department of State to provide briefings on antisemitism in Europe."
The title of the measure was amended. Agreed to without objection.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 1744.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 1744 — "To extend and authorize annual appropriations for the United States Commission on International Religious Freedom through fiscal year 2026."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 3429.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 3429 — "To establish an inter-parliamentary dialogue to facilitate closer cooperation between the United States, Japan, and the Republic of Korea on shared interests and values."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 2505.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Mast moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 2505 — "To require the development of strategies and options to prevent the export to Iran of certain technologies related to unmanned aircraft systems, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6028.
Considered under suspension of the rules.
Mr. Griffith moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 6028 — "To modify the appointment and removal process for certain legislative branch officers, and for other purposes."
The title of the measure was amended. Agreed to without objection.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 428.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 428 — "To amend title 5, United States Code, to enhance the authority under which Federal agencies may pay cash awards to employees for making cost saving disclosures, and for other purposes."

At the conclusion of debate, the Yeas and Nays were demanded and ordered. Pursuant to the provisions of clause 8, rule XX, the Chair announced that further proceedings on the motion would be postponed.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 8428.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 8428 — "To amend title 5, United States Code, to establish a mandatory antifraud and improper payment training program for Federal program administrators, to provide for the availability of such training to State and local entities administering Federally funded programs, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 8107.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 8107 — "To require the establishment of a list identifying program areas and administrative practices presenting the greatest risk to the integrity of Federal funds administered by States and local governments."

At the conclusion of debate, the chair put the question on the motion to suspend the rules. Mr. Gill (TX) objected to the vote on the grounds that a quorum was not present. Further proceedings on the motion were postponed. The point of no quorum was considered as withdrawn.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 8340.
Considered under suspension of the rules.
Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 8340 — "To modify the governmentwide financial management plan, and for other purposes."

At the conclusion of debate, the Yeas and Nays were demanded and ordered. Pursuant to the provisions of clause 8, rule XX, the Chair announced that further proceedings on the motion would be postponed.
Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 8466 — "To require certain agencies to develop plans for internal control in the event of an emergency or crisis, and for other purposes."
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 8466.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 8466 — "To require certain agencies to develop plans for internal control in the event of an emergency or crisis, and for other purposes."

At the conclusion of debate, the chair put the question on the motion to suspend the rules. Mr. Gill (TX) objected to the vote on the grounds that a quorum was not present. Further proceedings on the motion were postponed. The point of no quorum was considered as withdrawn.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 8467.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 8467 — "To reform the Payment Integrity Information Act of 2019 to ensure executive agencies focus on fraud prevention, and for other purposes."
The title of the measure was amended. Agreed to without objection.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6916.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 6916 — "To amend title 41, United States Code, to identify individuals who commit certain Federal felonies implicating Federal programs as an excluded source on the System for Award Management Exclusions list, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 8463.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Gill (TX) moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill, as amended. H.R. 8463 — "To establish governmentwide requirements for pre-payment fraud prevention actions, to provide the U.S. Treasury appropriate data resources, to facilitate participation in governmentwide anti-fraud data sharing, and for other purposes."
The Speaker announced that votes on suspensions, if ordered, will be postponed until a time to be announced.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of June 8.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.

The House received a message from the Clerk. Pursuant to the permission granted in Clause 2(h) of Rule II of the Rules of the U.S. House of Representatives, the Clerk notified the House that he had received the following message from the Secretary of the Senate on June 5, 2026, at 9:00 a.m.: That the Senate passed S. 2 and agreed to S. Res. 755. Appointment: Board of Trustees of the John C. Stennis Center for Public Service Training and Development.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches.
The Chair designated Ms. Davids of KS to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of June 8.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for 2:00 P.M. today.
The House proceeded with Morning-Hour Debate. At the conclusion of Morning-Hour, the House will recess until 2:00 p.m. for the start of legislative business.
The Speaker designated the Honorable Darrell Issa to act as Speaker pro tempore for today.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
